commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The main WeatherCard entry point component is missing from the source code.
issues.fix: Create a WeatherCard.tsx file that composes WeatherHero, WeatherMetrics, and ForecastStrip inside a Card or Surface primitive.

issues.description: Weather icon/emoji is missing from the main current weather display (Hero section), which was specifically requested.
issues.fix: Import and use the conditionIcon from the useComponent hook (or the iconMap) inside the WeatherHero component.

issues.description: The logic in hooks.ts is completely unused. Components take raw props instead of using the prepared formatted values.
issues.fix: Refactor WeatherHero and WeatherMetrics to use the useComponent hook to retrieve formatted strings and icons.

issues.description: Duplicate implementation of ForecastItem. It is defined as a standalone file and again as a local helper in ForecastStrip.tsx with different logic.
issues.fix: Delete the local ForecastItem in ForecastStrip.tsx and import the standalone version.

issues.description: Weather emojis lack aria-hidden or descriptive labels, which can be confusing for screen readers.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a span with role="img" and provide an aria-label or use aria-hidden="true" if the text condition is nearby.

evaluations.critique: The sub-components follow the design system well and have a clean visual hierarchy. However, the implementation is fundamentally incomplete as the root WeatherCard component is missing, the requested weather icon is absent from the hero section, and there is significant code duplication and unused logic in the hooks.
